articles.title: Hegel's Preface to the Phenomenology — and the Vedāntic parallels
articles.subtitle: Substanz als Subjekt, Aufhebung, the speculative proposition — placed against Bhartṛprapañca, Madhva, Vyāsatīrtha, Jīva Gosvāmī, Vidyāraṇya.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — first investigation
articles.subtitle: Testing three precisifications of the hypothesis that Pūrva-Mīmāṃsā ritual is the externalized form of an inner Vedic yogic process Aurobindo decodes in *The Secret of the Veda*.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — re-investigation defending the Integral-Yoga thesis
articles.subtitle: Re-do with stronger verdict: the Mīmāṃsaka apparatus has substantial interior content (Śabara on MS 1.1.2: *so 'rthaḥ puruṣaṃ niḥśreyasena saṃyunaktīti pratijānīmahe*) that the first pass underweighted. Clooney 2022's *Mīmāṃsā as Introspective Literature and as Philosophy* provides academic precedent. Sureśvara *NS* 1.67's structural-incapability critique lands against the externalized form, not the original integral form.
articles.superseded_note: v2's diagnostic-decline framing (Hypothesis C: 'the decline was the loss of the depth-reading') misrepresented the user's position. v3 retracts that framing and reframes Aurobindo's hermeneutic as operating on the contemporary reader's encounter with the corpus, not as a correction of the historical school.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as a coherent practice-philosophy: Clooney's reading and the contemporary reader's encounter
articles.subtitle: Replaces v2's diagnostic-decline framing. The classical Mīmāṃsakas externalised the rite because the symbol-environment they inhabited carried the depth in lived practice; the school is coherent on its own terms. Clooney's *Thinking Ritually* (1990) and 2022 "Mīmāṃsā as Introspective Literature and as Philosophy" are engaged substantively (codanā, bhāvanā, adhikāra, apūrva, the karmin as reflective subject). Aurobindo's *Secret of the Veda* and *Synthesis of Yoga* are reframed as a hermeneutic for the contemporary reader at distance from the original symbol-environment — amplification, not correction.
articles.superseded_note: v3 over-corrected v2's diagnostic-decline framing by retracting the symbolism-shift thesis entirely. v4 reinstates the symbolism-shift thesis as a structural-cultural fact, not a moral or school-internal decline narrative.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā worked: the symbol-environment thesis and the contemporary reader's encounter
articles.subtitle: Reinstates v2's symbolism-shift thesis cleanly, in its proper register, while retaining v3's correct insistence on Mīmāṃsā's coherence on its own terms. The school worked for the practitioners who inhabited the original symbol-environment; the symbol-environment changed across centuries as a structural cultural-historical fact (Religionsgeschichte, not moral decline); for participants outside the original substrate, the rite's operations remain analysable on the school's own terms but no longer carry their original immediate spiritual-symbolic register; Aurobindo's hermeneutic is amplification of what was once lived for the contemporary reader, not correction of a defective school. §5 explicitly rejects the school-internal decline narrative (Brahmin corruption, Sanskrit-fluency decay, kali-yuga) as a misdiagnosis of structural-cultural change as moral failure.

articles.title: An analytic-primitive model of Vedāntic and adjacent positions
articles.subtitle: Nine structural primitives — substrate-independence, mode-status, identity-relation, causation, locus-of-avidyā, bheda-count, epistemic-authority, mokṣa-state, bhakti-role — and the derivation table per school.

articles.title: The comparative-claims framework — methodology
articles.subtitle: Four-verdict schema for cross-school comparison: shared-presupposition / parallel-structure / terminological-equivalence / genuine-disagreement / contested. Five claim categories. Anti-pattern guards.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* with Tattva-Prakāśikā, Tātparya-Candrikā, Nyāya-Sudhā, Parimaḷa
articles.subtitle: A walk-through of Madhva's BSB across three commentarial layers — the bheda-pañcaka of Tattvavāda, the realism-recovery move on BS 1.1.1, the constructive thesis that *mithyā ≠ asat*.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Bhāgavata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya*
articles.subtitle: Madhva's settling-of-purport reading of the *Bhāgavata Purāṇa* — the bhakti axis of Tattvavāda, the gradation of jīvas, the locus of Viṣṇu's primacy in scripture.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhva's *Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana*
articles.subtitle: The polemical demolition of Advaitic māyāvāda — Madhva's argument that *mithyā* construed as ontological non-existence is incoherent, and the constructive corollary for the realism-recovery trajectory.

articles.title: Engagement: Nimbārka's *Vedānta-Pārijāta-Saurabha* with Śrīnivāsa's *Vedānta-Kaustubha*
articles.subtitle: Dvaitādvaita's parallel-realism reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — bheda and abheda as equiprimordial, the cit-acit-Brahman triad, and where Nimbārka sits between Rāmānuja and Madhva.

articles.title: Engagement: Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Śuddhādvaita as a third realism-Vedānta — the world as Brahman's *avirbhāva-tirobhāva* (manifestation-veiling), the rejection of pariṇāma and vivarta, and the bhakti-axis of pure non-duality.

articles.title: Engagement: Vedānta-Deśika's *Pāñcarātra-Rakṣā*
articles.subtitle: Deśika's defence of the scriptural authority of the Pāñcarātra against Mīmāṃsaka and Smārta attack — the tantric-Vaiṣṇava locus of Viśiṣṭādvaita's ritual ontology.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāghavendra's *Nyāya-Sudhā-Parimaḷa* (4 vols)
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's gloss on Jayatīrtha's *Nyāya-Sudhā* on Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna* — the late-Mādhva polemic apparatus deployed across all four adhyāyas of the Brahma-Sūtra.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāghavendra's *Tantra-Dīpikā*
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's commentary on Madhva's *Tantra-Sāra-Saṅgraha* — the Mādhva ritual-philosophical interface, mantra ontology, and the bhakti-jñāna integration.

articles.title: Engagement: Vidyāraṇya's *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṅgraha*
articles.subtitle: The Vivaraṇa-school digest — śravaṇa as ascertainment of textual purport, manana, nididhyāsana, the *mānasī-vṛtti* whose object is *pratyag-brahmaikya*, and the locus of Brahman as both *āśraya* and *viṣaya* of avidyā.

articles.title: Engagement: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's avibhāgādvaita — non-duality-by-non-difference rather than identity, the synthesis of Sāṃkhya and Vedānta, and the late-medieval reframing of jīva-Brahman relation.

articles.title: Engagement: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Yoga-Vārttika*
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's gloss on Vyāsa's *Yoga-Bhāṣya* — the practice-philosophy of Pātañjala Yoga read into Vedāntic ontology, the place of Īśvara, the kleśa-vāsanā architecture.

articles.title: Engagement: Yāmuna's *Āgama-Prāmāṇya*
articles.subtitle: Yāmuna's defence of the Pāñcarātra Āgama as scripturally authoritative — the founding text of the Viśiṣṭādvaita line, refuting the Mīmāṃsaka rejection of non-Vedic ritual literature.

articles.title: Engagement: Madhusūdana Sarasvatī's *Bhakti-Rasāyana*
articles.subtitle: Madhusūdana's reconciliation of bhakti with Advaitic non-dualism — bhakti as a *rasa* and a distinct *puruṣārtha*, the relation to *Bhāgavata*, and the bridge from Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* to a non-dual devotionalism.

articles.title: Engagement: Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya*
articles.subtitle: The full Viśiṣṭādvaita reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — the *mahā-pūrva-pakṣa* against Advaita on BS 1.1.1, the *aprākṛta śarīraka* construal of jīva-Brahman, and the architecture of qualified non-duality.
